title: silene muscipula var. oranensis
intro: Silene muscipula var. oranensis, also known as Catchfly or Catchfly Orchid, is a flowering plant species within the silene genus. This plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of anthraquinones and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Silene muscipula var. oranensis include its potential to treat gastrointestinal issues, such as constipation and diarrhea, due to its laxative and anti-inflammatory properties. Herbal preparations of this plant are often made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and can be used to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms associated with irritable bowel syndrome.
